subject: [PATCH v5 23/36] mm/mempolicy: apply policy at the kernel zone for private-node binds

2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch addresses a livelock issue in the Linux kernel's memory policy system by introducing a new function, `policy_private_has_kernel_zone`, which checks if any private node in a policy can satisfy non-movable allocations. The function is used to determine whether to apply the policy at the kernel zone for private-node binds. This change ensures that kernel allocations are not forced onto zones that cannot satisfy them, preventing infinite retries and improving system performance.
